what_it_does: Registers a single MCP tool named `flows` that exposes a file-discovered registry of repo-authored, multi-step production-line flows through an `op` selector (list/describe/run/propose).
observations: One tool with an `op` selector instead of a separate tool per flow.
Discovers the runnable set from `flows/<id>.py` on every call via `runtime.flows.FlowRegistry`.
All registered flows are required to declare `proposes_only=True` at discovery.
The `run` op invokes a flow by id with a params dict and rejects unknown parameter names.
The `propose` op accepts a flow spec including a `body` string of run() code but only surfaces it for operator approval; it refuses bodies that touch operator verbs or process launches.
An empty `list` result includes a note redirecting to the saved-cascades registry.
The docstring repeatedly states that flows emit no resolve/approve/dispatch and launch no `claude -p` process.
standouts: Op-selector tool shape (`list`/`describe`/`run`/`propose`) consolidating flow access.
Per-call file-system discovery of the flow registry (`flows/<id>.py`).
Propose gate: code bodies are screened and then held for operator source approval rather than applied.
Strict `proposes_only` floor: flows produce artifacts/review items, never final approvals or dispatches.
conventions: Follows an MCP design principle of one resource plus an operation selector rather than flat tool proliferation.
Heavy docstring-first policy documentation inside the tool definition.
Delegates actual registry logic and discovery to `runtime.flows.FlowRegistry`.
concerns: `propose` accepts executable code as a string (`spec.body`) with only surface-level screening visible here; actual sandboxing/execution mechanics are not shown.
Parameter validation is mentioned but only unknown-name rejection is visible; defaults, types, and schema enforcement live elsewhere.
`FlowRegistry().discover()` is called on every invocation, which could be expensive if the registry is large.
`sorted(reg)` and `sorted(reg.rows())` assume the registry object is iterable in two different ways without local definition.
Policy enforcement phrases (e.g., `proposes_only=True`, no dispatch) are asserted in the docstring but the local code does not show the enforcement implementation.
notes: This file is the thin MCP tool wrapper around a file-discovered flow registry, emphasizing operator-gated authorship and a strict propose-only execution floor.
questions: How does `FlowRegistry.discover()` map files to rows and enforce `proposes_only=True`?
What exactly does `suite.propose_flow(spec)` return and how is operator approval surfaced?
How is the `body` code sandboxed or executed when a proposed flow is later approved?
What mechanism enforces time budgets and resume-safe behavior mentioned for `run`?
How are declared parameter defaults, descriptions, and types validated beyond rejecting unknown names?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches what the file does: it registers a single `flows` tool for listing, describing, running, and proposing grounded production-line flows.
intention_signals: It appears designed to make proven, repo-committed automation chains callable through one MCP tool surface while ensuring new chains can only be added through human operator approval and remain strictly proposal-generating.
novelty: The combination of per-call file-system discovery of Python module flows, enforced `proposes_only` semantics, and a propose op that treats code submission as a human-gated source proposal rather than an immediate install is unusual.
contribution: It contributes a unified, op-selector MCP interface for bounded, repo-authored, propose-only flows with a visible human-approval gate for extending the registry.
summary_for_embedding: `mcp_face/tools/flows.py` registers the `flows` MCP tool that lists, describes, runs, and proposes file-discovered, propose-only production-line flows from a repo-authored registry.
intention_for_embedding: The file intends to give callers single-call access to grounded multi-step chains while keeping flow authorship committed, bounded, and operator-approved.

symbols.does: Registers the `flows` MCP tool on the provided `mcp` instance using the supplied `suite`.
symbols.description: This function is the module's entry point: it attaches a single `flows` tool with an `op` selector to the MCP server. It receives `mcp` and `suite` objects and uses them to expose list/describe/run/propose operations.

symbols.does: The MCP tool implementation that dispatches `list`, `describe`, `run`, and `propose` operations against the discovered flow registry.
symbols.description: It instantiates `runtime.flows.FlowRegistry`, calls `discover()`, and routes each `op`: returning flow summaries, a single flow spec, a proposal submission via `suite.propose_flow(spec)`, or the result of invoking a flow's `run()` with validated params. It raises `ValueError` for missing required arguments or unknown operations.
